sentence: Subjective:

The patient is here for an acne follow-up, having been previously seen on 3/3/2021 and prescribed clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream 4 to 5%, benzoyl peroxide wash for face and 5 to 10% for chest and back.
The patient reports using the prescribed medications and sunscreen as directed, with no redness, peeling, dryness, or irritation noted.
The patient mentions washing their face and chest/back with benzoyl peroxide and applying tretinoin cream and sunscreen at night.
The patient states the acne on their face is improving and is happy with their current treatment plan.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of acne, for which they are currently being treated.
Review of systems:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
I prescribed clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream 4 to 5% for acne on the face, benzoyl peroxide wash 4 to 5% for the face, and 5 to 10% for the chest and back, and sunscreen as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has scattered inflammatory papules and pustules across the upper back and posterior shoulders, with less than five inflammatory papules on the forehead, cheeks, and chin.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's acne is improved and well-controlled on the face but has a mild flare on the back.
Problem list: Acne vulgaris on the face; Acne vulgaris on the back; [NO OTHER PROBLEMS MENTIONED]
Plan:
I recommend continuing the current treatment plan for the face and initiating treatment with Panoxyl 10% foaming wash to the back in the shower daily.
Follow-up: The patient should return in one year for a follow-up appointment, at which time their treatment plan will be re-evaluated.
